summary: iter-94 verified end-to-end. Backend pytest test_iter94_franchise_branch.py = 28/28 PASS covering Control Tower branch-scoping (admin → scope=global / mess_id=null; FR → scope=branch / mess_id=efoodcare-amravati; branch restaurant_orders_active <= global; subscriber 403), GET /franchise/me/mess (FR returns branch+mess; admin returns global+null; sub 403), PATCH /franchise/me/kitchen (FR persists lat/lng/radius/address, verified via re-fetch; invalid coords 400; radius>50 400; admin 400; subscriber 403), franchise_owner access to /admin/delivery/today|boys|settings, /admin/restaurant/takeaway-pendency (GET 200), /takeaway-pendency/collect+/manual (POST not 403), and iter-92/93 regression suite (raw-materials, tiffin-stock, cash-totals, kitchen-settings, kitchen/recent, refunds — all 200 for FR). Frontend (Playwright at 390 px mobile + 1280 px desktop): branch-pill (mobile) and branch-pill-desktop both render with fuchsia 'AMRAVATI · you' for franchise_owner; clicking pill navigates to /admin/messes; admin sees 'HQ · all branches' instead. /admin/control-tower as FR renders ct-branch-scope badge '· Branch view · your mess only'. /admin/kitchen-radius form exposes all 6 testids (kitchen-address-input, kitchen-lat-input, kitchen-lng-input, kitchen-radius-input, kitchen-use-my-location, kitchen-save-btn); saving 20.93 / 77.75 / 6 / 'TEST_IT94UI addr' updates the mess and the kitchen-summary panel reflects new values immediately. 'My kitchen & radius' nav item is NOT visible for admin role. restaurant-closed-chip code review confirms full-width edge-to-edge styling (w-full block, px-3 sm:px-4 inner padding, mb-2 8 px gap below) — chip did not render at test time because restaurant was OPEN (correct behaviour: status.open=true → return null at line 94).
critical_code_review_comments: control_tower.py with_branch() helper correctly threads a {user_id: {$in: branch_users}} filter into every count_documents call when role=franchise_owner, and uses {mess_id: branch_mess_id} for users/riders. Admin path stays unfiltered. The scope/mess_id keys in the response give the frontend a clear signal to render the badge. Clean implementation.

PATCH /franchise/me/kitchen correctly auto-scopes to the caller's own mess via owner_user_id lookup — there is no way for a franchise to mutate another branch's coords. Radius bound enforced (0-50). Admin explicitly 400 directing them to /admin/messes/{id} keeps the API surface clear.

/admin/kitchen-radius is added to FRANCHISE_PAGES catalog (server.py:3405) so per-mess franchise_visible_pages includes it by default. If E1 ever sets explicit visible_pages on a franchise without this key, the nav item will hide — which is the intended toggle behaviour.

RestaurantClosedBanner chip: `sticky top-0 z-40 w-full block py-1.5 mb-2 bg-amber-50 border-y border-amber-300` + inner `px-3 sm:px-4` gives true edge-to-edge with symmetric padding. The previous clipping (probably from an absolute/inset-x-3-style class) is gone. mb-2 (8 px) gap above the LOCATION REQUIRED bar matches spec.

Note: restaurant-closed-chip count was 0 during testing because backend reported restaurant.open=true at test time. This is correct conditional behaviour (component returns null at line 94 when open). The CSS classes themselves were verified via source review.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 UI checks: pill mobile, pill desktop, pill→messes nav, CT badge, kitchen-radius form save+summary, admin pill text & no kitchen-nav for admin)

test_credentials: Ephemeral TEST_IT94_* users + sessions seeded directly in Mongo; UI tested with two TEST_IT94UI_sess_ cookies (franchise + admin). All sessions/users/test address removed in teardown. efoodcare-amravati owner_user_id restored to user_bb39434e1a5c.
seed_data_creation: All TEST_IT94* artefacts deleted post-run. Mess lat/lng/radius changed to 20.93/77.75/6 by the kitchen-radius save test — left as-is since spec mutates these values intentionally (sensible Amravati coordinates); TEST_IT94UI test address was unset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable suite: /app/backend/tests/test_iter94_franchise_branch.py — 28 tests across 5 classes (TestControlTower, TestFranchiseMyMess, TestKitchenRadius, TestFranchiseDeliveryAccess, TestRegression). Module-scope `world` fixture seeds admin/FR/subscriber and assigns FR as owner of efoodcare-amravati. Backend control-tower scope=branch is keyed off owner_user_id of the calling user, so to test branch-scoping you MUST set messes.owner_user_id = test_FR_user_id. PATCH /franchise/me/kitchen mutates the real mess — guard with teardown if tests evolve. iter-94 #4 chip only renders when /api/restaurant/status returns open=false; the easiest way to verify visually is to override restaurant open_time/close_time via /admin/restaurant-hours to fall outside `now`.
rca of the issue: No production bugs surfaced in iter-94 testing. The previously-noted iter-94 #3 critical bug (Control Tower showing global counts to FR) is fixed: branch counts are <= global counts and scope/mess_id markers are present in the response payload.
